title: Community
text:
Machine Learning Systems is taught at 40+ universities across six continents — from Bogotá to Johannesburg, from Kobe to Nairobi.
  Every chapter and lab is open and free — and we ship hardware kits to partner universities that need them.
  AI has no borders. Neither should the education to build it.

title: Community
text:
Beyond the Textbook
Every chapter is free to read — but access means more than a URL. It means hardware, training, and showing up in person.

  
    
    Global Academic Network
    40+ universities across six continents teaching ML systems — from Harvard and Columbia to institutions across Africa, Asia, and Latin America. Through our partnership with the International Centre for Theoretical Physics (ICTP) in Trieste, Italy, we provide curriculum, instructor training, and ongoing support.
  
  
    
    Hardware Where It's Needed
    We ship real hardware — Arduino and Seeed edge devices — to universities that couldn't otherwise afford them. Every partner institution receives kits so students can learn on real silicon, not just simulations.
  
  
    
    Workshops on the Ground
    The annual SciTinyML workshop series at ICTP in Trieste brings researchers and students together to apply ML to real scientific challenges — from environmental monitoring to health diagnostics in resource-constrained settings.
  
  
    
    Free and Open — Always
    The full two-volume textbook is available as free HTML, PDF, and EPUB. The HarvardX TinyML Professional Certificate on edX reaches thousands of self-paced learners worldwide. CC-BY-NC-SA licensed so anyone can teach from it.

title: Community
text:
Join Us

  
    
    Discussions
    Ask questions, report issues, and discuss chapters with the community and authors.
  
  
    
    Newsletter
    Updates from the author's desk — new chapters, curriculum news, and community spotlights.
  
  
    
    Contribute
    Submit pull requests, improve chapters, build labs, or review content.
